seoTitle: Stony Creek vs Stony Crossing — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Stony Creek and Stony Crossing. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Stony Creek vs Stony Crossing: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stony Creek and Stony Crossing.
heading: Stony Creek vs Stony Crossing — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stony Creek, New South Wales and Stony Crossing, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stony Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Stony Creek (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stony Creek or Stony Crossing safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stony Creek has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Stony Crossing scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stony Creek scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Stony Crossing scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stony Creek or Stony Crossing?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stony Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
